content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Freelancers: Build Faster with AI
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier. Master SaaS development using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional apps for your clients in record time.
content.answer_block: Stop building from scratch. Use a production-ready SaaS starter kit to bypass boilerplate hurdles. By integrating Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can transform your freelance workflow, shipping high-converting SaaS applications in days rather than months, regardless of your current technical background.
content.intro:
The freelance landscape has shifted. Clients no longer pay for hours; they pay for finished, functional SaaS products that move the needle. You're staring at a daunting stack of authentication, database schema, and payment integration, paralyzed by the 'no SaaS knowledge' wall. It feels like you're trying to build a skyscraper without a blueprint.

But the era of manual coding is dead. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your agency. We aren't just writing code; we are orchestrating systems. This guide bridges the gap between being a freelancer and a SaaS architect, using Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Establish the base architecture using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to ensure your project starts with production-grade security and structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular SaaS starter repository with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase integration. Include a robust directory structure for API routes and database models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize starter kit project structure using Antigravity standards'

steps.title: Configure Authentication and User Roles
steps.description: Implement enterprise-grade auth. Avoid custom logic by using standardized patterns that handle RBAC and session management out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up authentication flows for user sign-up, login, and protected dashboard routes using Supabase Auth and middleware guards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement secure authentication middleware and protected route guards'

steps.title: Connect Data Models
steps.description: Map your client's business logic to database schemas. This step ensures your app is data-driven and scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Prisma schema definitions for a multi-tenant SaaS application including Users, Subscriptions, and Project entities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate Prisma schema and migrate database for multi-tenant SaaS'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Billing
steps.description: Revenue is the heartbeat of SaaS. Use automated billing logic to handle subscription tiers and webhooks without manual coding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Stripe webhook handler to sync subscription status with the database and restrict dashboard access based on plan tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure Stripe checkout session and webhook subscription listener'

steps.title: Deploy and Automate CI/CD
steps.description: Ship to production with automated pipelines. Remove the manual deployment friction that kills freelance productivity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel-ready CI/CD configuration that runs linting and type-checking on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Set up automated deployment pipeline for production environment'

pro_tips: Always use a monorepo structure to keep your UI and API code synchronized, reducing context switching.
Hard-code your environment variables into a template file to prevent 'it works on my machine' errors with clients.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth middleware first; it prevents 90% of production security bugs.
Standardize your component library early; changing styles across 50 pages is the fastest way to lose profit margin.
Automate your database migrations using a CLI tool to ensure your client's production data never drifts from your development environment.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, focus on implementing user feedback loops. Use the time saved by your starter kit to build custom features that differentiate your client's SaaS from the competition.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the same architecture patterns used by top-tier SaaS engineering teams. Don't let technical debt dictate your freelance career—master the stack and build products that scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
